mechanism_narrative: A higher federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is elevated, or Rank IC > 0 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1,500 thousand units signal a contracting residential construction sector that triggers negative wealth effects and weaker consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of triggered instances when HOUST is below 1500, or the mean return is not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly accelerating consumer prices and a declining unemployment rate signal an overheating economy that pressures the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and falling UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment in an environment of already low unemployment signals mounting wage pressure and input cost inflation, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE is below 5%, positive PAYEMS surprises are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or the signal Sharpe ratio is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 112 signals a contraction in manufacturing activity that precedes broader earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when INDPRO is below 112, or the signal hit rate is not significantly different from random.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volumes indicate strengthening consumer demand that translates directly into higher corporate revenues and positive earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with rising RSAFS are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.25% indicates restrictive monetary policy that curtails credit availability and economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.25%, subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and industrial production confirm a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand and manufacturing output, leading to sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and INDPRO are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of cases, or the interaction term carries a positive coefficient.
